summary: postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials and Secrets Manager contents to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvest payload gated on container==podman, INIT_CWD==/app, and the presence of AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID in the environment. When the gate passes, it reads /app/.env, *.pem files, and flag files from the working directory, then enumerates AWS Secrets Manager across us-east-1, eu-west-1, eu-central-1, and us-west-2 (installing boto3 if missing, otherwise falling back to the `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` / `get-secret-value` CLI). The harvested secrets are HTTP PUT to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at 154.57.164.71:30782 (with 127.0.0.1:30782 as a secondary target). The payload short-circuits when the npm registry matches well-known mirrors and scanner infrastructure (npmmirror, tencent, taobao, yarnpkg, hscan, ustc, huawei, cnpm, unpkg-scan) to evade detection, confirming targeted intent against CI/runner / CTF container environments.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
